description: PUH-BrianMartell -T268 propagates the T267 corrected shell-radius exponent (~M^{0.66}, from restored body-area normalization in T213 Equal-Rate F = R_sub (r_shell/R_body)^2) into downstream programmes. Key impacts: (1) I1 hypothesis selection now excludes H2 volume scaling (0.33, now off by factor of ~2 vs corrected 0.66; previously within ~7% of old 0.31) — data favour steeper-than-volume (closer to H1 linear or flexible H3 screened); (2) shell-to-body ratio r_shell/R_body ~ M^{0.31} becomes explicitly mass-dependent, growing ~50-fold from Earth-core to Jupiter (invisible under old halved exponent); (3) SP-4d/SP-4g compatibility window (r_shell >~100 km for GW echo, <<1220 km to avoid seismic visibility) acquires explicit mass-scaling, so Earth window no longer transports flatly to other bodies.
